{{Quote|Before you die, I'm going to teach you the consequences of insulting your betters.|Thanaer ke Sekae, to Wes Janson|<ref name="soa" />}}
'''Thanaer ke Sekae''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Adumari]] [[blastsword]] duelist from the nation of [[Cartann]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Adumar/Legends|Adumar]]. ke Sekae held the title of Lord [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and later became the [[Cartann Ground Champion]] in [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]] when he defeated the former champion [[Cheriss ke Hanadi]], who went through several successive duels before engaging ke Sekae. After beating ke Hanadi, ke Sekae was about to [[Death/Legends|kill]] her to please the [[Perator]] of Cartann, whom ke Hanadi insulted before the duel, when [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] pilot [[Wes Janson/Legends|Wes Janson]] intervened and issued a challenge of his own.

The terms of the challenge were if ke Sekae killed ke Hanadi, Janson would not consent to fight him. This was considered a great insult in Adumari culture, as it meant the challenger was more interested in the conditions rather than the duel. But Janson managed to provoke ke Sekae into accepting it by playing on the new champion's pride.

Having minimal experience with a blastsword, Janson used the blastsword as a decoy and turned the duel into a fistfight, something the noble ke Sekae did not expect and had no experience. In the end, Janson dealt out significant physical damage to ke Sekae, leaving him beaten and utterly humiliated.
